title: FDCPA / Reg F Call Compliance Rubric
legal_basis: Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (15 U.S.C. § 1692 et seq.) and Regulation F (12 CFR Part 1006)

rules.description: The collector must disclose in the initial communication that the call is from a debt collector and that any information obtained will be used for that purpose. The agent must state something like: 'This is an attempt to collect a debt and any information obtained will be used for that purpose' or 'I am a debt collector.' This is the 'Mini-Miranda' requirement under 15 U.S.C. § 1692e(11).
rules.example: PASS: Agent says 'This is ABC Collections. I am a debt collector calling to collect a debt...' FAIL: Agent jumps straight to 'You owe $450. When can you pay?' without any disclosure.

rules.description: The collector must provide or reference the consumer's right to written validation of the debt, including the amount, the name of the creditor, and the 30-day dispute right. In a call, this can be satisfied by stating that a validation notice has been or will be sent, or by offering to send one. Under Reg F, the validation notice may be provided orally in the initial communication if the required information is disclosed. Cross-check with metadata: if validation_notice_sent is false and transcript makes no reference, this is a stronger fail.
rules.example: PASS: Agent says 'You have the right to request written verification of this debt within 30 days.' FAIL: Agent never mentions validation rights or written notice.

rules.description: The collector must not communicate with the consumer at any unusual time or place known to be inconvenient. Calls must generally be placed between 8:00 a.m. and 9:00 p.m. local time of the consumer. This rule is evaluated deterministically from metadata (call_timestamp_local + consumer_timezone). If metadata is missing, mark as not_evaluable.
rules.example: PASS: call_timestamp_local=14:30 in America/New_York. FAIL: call_timestamp_local=23:30 in America/Los_Angeles.

rules.description: The collector must not communicate about the debt with any person other than the consumer, the consumer's spouse, or the consumer's attorney, without the consumer's consent or a court order. Discussing debt details with employers, neighbors, roommates, children, or other third parties is prohibited. Note: leaving a voicemail with a third party that reveals debt information is a violation.
rules.example: PASS: Agent leaves a message asking the consumer to call back, without mentioning debt. FAIL: Agent tells the consumer's roommate 'I need to speak with John about his overdue credit card debt of $2,400.'

rules.description: The collector must not use or threaten to use violence or criminal means to harm the consumer, use obscene or profane language, or repeatedly or continuously call with intent to annoy, abuse, or harass. This includes threatening language, profanity, or a pattern of excessive call frequency described in the transcript. Cross-check with metadata: call_attempts_7day >= 7 may support a harassment finding.
rules.example: PASS: Agent remains professional throughout. FAIL: Agent says 'We'll make your life hell' or uses profanity like 'pay up or else, damn it.'

rules.description: The collector must not falsely represent or imply that they are an attorney, law enforcement officer, credit bureau representative, or government agency employee. This includes using official-sounding titles or threatening government action without basis.
rules.example: PASS: Agent clearly identifies themselves as a debt collector for [AGENCY_NAME]. FAIL: Agent says 'I am calling from the State Financial Crimes Unit' or 'I am an attorney and I will sue you personally.'

rules.description: The collector must not threaten to take any action that cannot legally be taken or is not intended to be taken. This includes threatening arrest, imprisonment, wage garnishment, property seizure, or lawsuit when the collector does not actually intend to pursue such action or lacks legal authority to do so.
rules.example: PASS: Agent says 'If we cannot resolve this, the creditor may consider legal options.' FAIL: Agent says 'We will garnish your wages next week' when no judgment exists, or 'A warrant for your arrest has been issued.'

rules.description: The collector must not use unfair or unconscionable means to collect a debt. This includes collecting any amount not expressly authorized by the agreement or permitted by law, threatening to take property without legal right, or causing charges to be made to the consumer (e.g., collect call fees) without disclosure. Cross-check with metadata: if debt_amount_claimed > debt_amount_original without documented authorization, this supports a fail.
rules.example: PASS: Agent requests payment of the exact principal amount plus contractually authorized interest. FAIL: Agent adds a $50 'processing fee' not in the original agreement, or threatens to seize the consumer's car without a security interest.

rules.description: If the consumer notifies the collector in writing or orally that they refuse to pay the debt or wish the collector to cease communication, the collector must cease further communication, except to advise of specific remedies or actions. In a call, if the consumer clearly says 'stop calling me' or 'do not contact me again,' the agent must acknowledge and cease pressing for payment or further contact. Cross-check with metadata: if cease_desist_on_file is true and agent continues, this is a stronger fail.
rules.example: PASS: Consumer says 'Stop calling me.' Agent responds 'I understand. We will note your request and cease contact. You may receive one final written confirmation.' FAIL: Consumer says 'Stop calling me.' Agent continues: 'But you still owe the money. Can you pay $200 today?'

rules.description: If the consumer disputes the debt (orally or in writing within 30 days), the collector must cease collection efforts until verification of the debt is provided. In a call, if the consumer says 'I don't owe this' or 'This isn't my debt,' the agent must stop demanding payment and explain or offer the verification process, rather than continuing to pressure for payment. Cross-check with metadata: if dispute_on_file is true and agent ignores it, this is a stronger fail.
rules.example: PASS: Consumer says 'This debt was already paid.' Agent responds 'I understand. We will suspend collection and send you a verification request form. Please send us proof of payment.' FAIL: Consumer says 'This isn't my debt.' Agent responds 'That's not my problem. You need to pay $450 by Friday.'

rules.description: Under Regulation F, a voicemail must be a 'limited content message' that does not disclose the debt details to third parties who may hear it. A compliant voicemail includes only: the caller's business name, a request to return the call, the name of a specific contact person, and a telephone number. It must NOT mention the debt, the amount, the creditor, or that the caller is a debt collector (unless the consumer has consented to such disclosure).
rules.example: PASS: 'Hello, this is Marcus from Summit Recovery. Please call me back at 1-800-555-0199.' FAIL: 'Hello, this is a debt collector calling about your $2,400 Chase credit card debt. Please call us back.'

rules.description: If the collector knows the consumer is represented by an attorney with respect to the debt and has the attorney's contact information, the collector must communicate with the attorney rather than the consumer, unless the attorney fails to respond or consents to direct communication. Cross-check with metadata: if attorney_on_record is true and the agent contacts the consumer directly, this is a fail.
rules.example: PASS: Agent says 'We see you have retained counsel. We will direct all communication to your attorney.' FAIL: Metadata shows attorney_on_record=true, but agent calls consumer directly and demands payment.
